parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Restrict to entries logged by this user.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: Restrict to entries logged against this project. Combined with any user/manager scoping the actor is already subject to.

post.description: Creates one time entry. Returns 409 when the target user already has an entry for the same date, project, and task. Returns 403 when the selected project is in a status that blocks time entries, or when it is expired and the role lacks timesheets.expired_projects.create.

delete.description: Deletes entries matching `projectId` and `task`. Callers with only `timesheets.recurring.delete` are limited to placeholder entries, even if `placeholderOnly=false` is supplied; tracker delete permissions can delete all matching entries allowed by their scope.

post.summary: Generate time entries from recurring templates
post.description: Walks the active recurring task templates assigned to the target user (or the authenticated user if `userId` is omitted) and inserts a placeholder time entry for every matching day in `[fromDate, toDate]` that does not already have an entry for the same (date, project, task) tuple. Projects in statuses that block time entries are always skipped; expired projects are skipped unless the role has timesheets.expired_projects.create. Idempotent.

supplierQuoteId.description: Supplier quote id snapshot. The server derives this from supplierQuoteItemId and clears an id supplied without an item reference.

supplierQuoteItemId.description: Supplier quote item source. Without accounting.supplier_orders.create, creates require the accepted same-client source offer and may use each item only as many times as that offer does; updates may only retain references already stored on the order. Retained updates preserve stored supplier metadata. Version restore revalidates the live item and refreshes its supplier metadata.
